entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: A legume crop studied for the protective role of its seed coat against fungal infection.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: A fungus that causes aflatoxin contamination in food crops.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: A global menace affecting food crops and human health caused by A. flavus infection.

entities.name: SEED COAT
entities.description: The outer layer of the peanut seed that acts as a physical and biochemical barrier against pathogens.

entities.name: IN-VITRO SEED COLONIZATION
entities.description: A method used to investigate the role of the seed coat in preventing fungal colonization.

entities.name: RADIAL GROWTH BIOASSAY
entities.description: A bioassay used to evaluate the inhibition of A. flavus growth by seed coat extracts.
A resistant peanut line showing higher inhibition of A. flavus.

entities.name: TMV-2
entities.description: A susceptible peanut line showing lower inhibition of A. flavus.

entities.name: HYDROXYCINNAMIC ACID DERIVATIVES
entities.description: Predominant phenolic compounds in the seed coat that inhibit A. flavus growth.

entities.name: HYDROXYBENZOIC ACID DERIVATIVES
entities.description: Predominant phenolic compounds in the seed coat that inhibit A. flavus growth.

entities.name: GENE EXPRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: A method used to analyze the abundance of genes involved in biosynthesis pathways in seed coats.

entities.name: LIGNIN MONOMER BIOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: A biochemical process involved in the formation of the seed coat, more abundant in the 55-437 line.

entities.name: PROANTHOCYANIDIN BIOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: A biochemical process involved in the formation of the seed coat, more abundant in the 55-437 line.

entities.name: FLAVONOID BIOSYNTHESIS
entities.description: A biochemical process involved in the formation of the seed coat, more abundant in the 55-437 line.

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a peanut line to inhibit the growth and infection of A. flavus.
